title: Malware Analysis Lab
html:
The standard isolated-VM setup (typically VMware or VirtualBox, NAT'd guest, with Wireshark / Procmon / Process Hacker) used to safely run and observe malware samples. Yahir's spring-2025 CS security capstone built around this — June 2025 screenshots show him running suspicious binaries inside a 192.168.157.134 VMware NAT guest, capturing traffic, observing process behavior. Sits inside the broader reverse engineering thread alongside IDA Pro static analysis.

TL;DR

  • 22 inbound citations, almost all from the June 2025 CS-capstone cluster.
  • Standard tooling: VMware/VirtualBox NAT'd Windows guest + Wireshark + Procmon + Process Hacker.
  • Documented IP 192.168.157.134 (VMware NAT range) appears across multiple screenshots — the same lab VM running a series of samples.
  • Pairs with reverse engineering (static analysis side) — together they form Yahir's full-stack security analysis loop.
  • Adjacent to network forensics (the Wireshark-side discipline) and REMnux (the alternative Linux-side analysis distro).

Key facts

  • Isolated network is the precondition. NAT mode on the VM = malware can phone home to a controlled network without touching the host LAN.
  • /ned.exe HTTP fetch. 2025 06 10 screenshot 2025 06 10 at 1 05 42 am f1abc915 captures suspicious /ned.exe HTTP fetches alongside normal Windows update chatter — classic suspicious-binary signature.
  • June 9–11 peak density. Most of the lab work concentrated in three days during finals.
  • Static + dynamic combination. The same week as first IDA Pro screenshot (static analysis) — Yahir was running both disciplines in parallel.
  • Coursework, not personal project. The lab setup is OSU-issued; the curiosity is Yahir's.

Plus ~6 additional lab-session screenshots from late May / early June 2025.

Connections

  • reverse engineering · ida pro · busybox · webp cve · buffer overflow — full security cluster
  • wireshark · network forensics — traffic-side analysis
  • remnux · dvwa — adjacent lab tooling
  • cs coursework · osu — institutional context
  • python — analysis scripting
  • mobile malware — adjacent specialty
